Burmeisters' Motion To Compel Defendants Additional Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Things, Set 1 is granted and denied in part. RFP 63: The compound and relevancy objections are overruled. Defendants fail to provide any evidence to show how documents responsive to the request regarding the trust would violate defendants' individual privacy rights. Defendants shall produce: the trust agreement (if not already produced): a list of trustees, beneficiaries, and trust assets and liabilities (for a period of 2013 and 2014). The attorney-client privilege and work product objections are sustained. Defendants shall produce a privilege log listing the documents withheld under these privileges. RFP 67: The objection that the request violates CCP 2031.030(c)(1) is sustained. The request for all documents, tangible things and other items that support, refute, or in any relate to the allegations in paragraph 2 does not "reasonably particularizing each category of item." The Court finds that the parties met and conferred sufficiently. Amended responses and responsive documents shall be produced within three weeks of notice of entry of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
